fixtures.notes: REAL: 17.1.0 adds DataGrid.pagination, purely additive (defaults false, byte-identical render to 17.0.0). Built, tagged, and published to Verdaccio against the real Angular 17.3.12/PrimeNG 17.18/AG Grid 30.2.1 toolchain. orders-app-ng17 (angular/apps/ng-17/orders-app) is a real, production-buildable consumer app installing this from Verdaccio, verified end-to-end in a real Chrome instance. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL, verified regression: same defect as the ng15 line's data-grid-visual-regression — 17.2.0 wraps the AG Grid in a container div with 16px padding that didn't exist at 17.0.0/17.1.0, still Layer-0 clean (a var() reference). Confirmed in a real Chrome instance against the real Angular 17/AG Grid 30 toolchain, zero console errors. orders-app-ng17 (angular/apps/ng-17/orders-app) is a real, production-buildable consumer app installing the candidate from Verdaccio. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL, verified regression: same defect as the ng15 line's core-controls-multiselect-semantic-regression — 17.2.0 switches MultiSelect's optionValue from "value" to "label". Looks identical, emits the wrong data. Confirmed in a real Chrome instance: selecting "Open" emits "Open", not "open", zero console errors. This same 17.2.0 release ALSO carries a build/peer-dep break (see core-controls-peer-dep-break-ng17 below) — a real-world breaking release plausibly bundles more than one change. admin-app-ng17 (angular/apps/ng-17/admin-app) is a real, production-buildable consumer app installing core-controls from Verdaccio. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL, verified regression: same defect class as both Angular lines — 19.2.0 switches MultiSelect's optionValue from "value" to "label" (react/libraries/lib-r19/core-controls/src/RippleViewMultiSelect.tsx). Looks identical, emits the wrong data. Confirmed in a real Chrome instance via the lib-r19 playground: selecting "Open" emits "Open", not "open", zero console errors. This is the React side's deliberately reduced scope: ONE generation (react19 only, no react18 line), ONE bundled package (@op/react-core-controls — Button/Input/MultiSelect/Form, mirroring Angular's core-controls bundling decision), no DataGrid, no shared-services package, and TWO consumer apps only (orders-app, settings-app — no mid-migration twin, since there's only one generation). settings-app (react/apps/r-19/settings-app) is now a real, production-buildable standalone Vite app — its own package.json/vite.config.ts/node_modules, installing core-controls from Verdaccio — using MultiSelect (shared with orders-app) and Input (unique to settings-app). React exists to prove the framework's cross-framework agnosticism, not as a second primary target. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL: billing-app-ng15 (angular/apps/ng-15/billing-app) is a real, production-buildable consumer app, deliberately pinned to core-controls@15.0.0 (a literal package.json pin, the "currently deployed" straggler state) while 15.1.0/15.2.0 are real, published versions on the SAME ng15 line. No behavior change at 15.0.0 itself, so it still PASSES today, but drift score is high (design §8 drift formula: minorsBehind/patchesBehind/ageFactor) — proves drift and pass/fail verdict are independent signals. Fully real fixture, both halves.

orders-app exists as TWO real, independent app projects — angular/apps/ng-15/orders-app (this entry) and angular/apps/ng-17/orders-app (see ng17-orders-datagrid-compatible) — the mid-migration twin, simulating two git branches checked out side by side, each its own production-buildable consumer app. The ng15 copy is mid-migration, not yet cut over; its drift includes the 500x frameworkGenerationsBehind term (design §8) on top of ordinary version drift, even though it still passes. The registry MUST namespace these as two separate angular/15 vs angular/17 entries, never merged. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL: deliberately least-maintained, standalone consumer app (angular/apps/ng-15/brownfield-app, own package.json/angular.json/node_modules/.npmrc), pinned to core-controls@15.0.0 — the oldest published version on the ng15 line, several minors behind 15.2.0, real high drift. Contains exactly one deliberate, clearly-commented Layer-0 violation (a hardcoded hex border-color in app.component.scss instead of a semantic theme variable, G15) and has never been run through the gate (neverGated: true). Confirmed live in a real Chrome instance via getComputedStyle: the hardcoded color renders exactly as intended, zero console errors. The static gate must catch the Layer-0 violation; confidence resolves to Unknown (never gated => Unknown, not a number, design §8), not a fabricated score. README.md explicitly marks this app as an intentional bad-citizen fixture, never a reference. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL underlying defect, but here billing-app-ng17 (angular/apps/ng-17/billing-app, a real, production-buildable consumer app — ) has a linked accepted-bug waiver for this exact change (design §8: "accepted bugs count as known & passing"). Deliberately the SAME candidate version as the unaccepted regression in admin-app-ng17, consumed by a DIFFERENT app with a DIFFERENT waiver state — proves acceptance is a property of the consuming app's waiver, not the library version itself. The underlying finding is still recorded (findingClass stays 'semantic' — the diff is real and never hidden), but the WAIVED gate verdict is 'pass' and confidence is NOT depressed (testsPassed + acceptedBugs in the design's confidence formula). Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL, verified regression: 15.2.0 switches MultiSelect's optionValue from "value" to "label" (angular/libraries/lib-ng15/projects/core-controls/src/lib/rv-multi-select). The dropdown still shows the same options and still LOOKS correct, but the emitted selection now carries the display label (e.g. "Open") instead of the real value ("open") — a silent data/semantic bug, not a visual one. Confirmed in a real browser: selecting "Open" emits "Open", not "open", with zero console errors. core-controls bundles Button/Input/MultiSelect/Form as ONE published package (a deliberate, accepted divergence from this story's original one-component-per-package AC text), so this is the ng15 line's AC-4-required oracle-listed defect. orders-app-ng15 (angular/apps/ng-15/orders-app) is a real, production-buildable consumer app installing this candidate from Verdaccio, verified end-to-end in a real Chrome instance. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL, verified regression: 15.2.0 wraps the AG Grid in a container div with non-zero padding (angular/libraries/lib-ng15/projects/data-grid/src/lib/data-grid.component.ts) — 15.0.0/15.1.0 had no wrapper at all, so this is a genuine, unexpected shift in the grid's visual bounds (the AC's own suggested example defect class), still Layer-0 clean (a var() reference, never a hardcoded literal). Confirmed in a real browser: wrapper padding is 16px where there was none before, zero console errors. orders-app-ng15 (angular/apps/ng-15/orders-app) is a real, production-buildable consumer app installing this candidate from Verdaccio. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL, verified build/peer-dep break: 17.2.0 bumps core-controls's @angular/core (and @angular/common, @angular/forms) peerDependency to ^18.0.0, while orders-app-ng17 (angular/apps/ng-17/orders-app, a real, production-buildable consumer app) stays pinned to @angular/core 17.3.12. Verified for real, not just declared: packed the published 17.2.0 tarball and installed it against a throwaway package.json pinned to @angular/core 17.3.12 — npm's strict peer-dep resolution hard-failed with a real ERESOLVE error (not a warning). The app will not even install/build against the candidate -> confidence MUST be exactly 0 (design §8), reported as a finding, never a crash (G10). Same candidate version ALSO carries the real multiselect semantic regression (see ng17-admin-multiselect-semantic-regression) — a consumer who can't even install it never gets far enough to see that bug. Fully real fixture, both halves.

REAL: 19.1.0 adds RippleViewButton's variant prop, purely additive (defaults 'primary', byte-identical render to 19.0.0). Built, tagged, and published to Verdaccio against the real React 19.2.7/PrimeReact 10.9.3 toolchain. orders-app (react/apps/r-19/orders-app) is a real, production-buildable, fully standalone consumer app installing this from Verdaccio, verified end-to-end in a real Chrome instance — MultiSelect filtering its order list, Button refresh action. Mirrors ng17-orders-datagrid-compatible's role on the Angular side: proves no false positive on a safe upgrade, cross-framework. Fully real fixture, both halves.
